title: Intent
description: An Intent is a classifier-produced event the bridge surfaces to an agent's inbox. Produced by a classifier's `classify()` from a raw `webhook_events` row. JSONL-encoded one-per-line in `<state_dir>/inbox.jsonl`.

kind.description: Dotted intent name. Conventional shape: `<resource>_<lifecycle>` (e.g. `new_card`, `card_moved`). The producer's classifier owns this namespace; consumers shouldn't assume any fixed set.

subject_id.description: Stable identifier of the subject the intent is about (e.g., a card id, a PR number, a comment id). Used by handlers for debounce keying and by consumers for cross-event correlation.

provider.description: Upstream system that produced the event (`kanban`, `github`, etc.). Stamped by the receiver from the per-adapter URL (`?p=<provider>`); copied through by classifiers. Schema version 2+ — the wire-level source of truth for provider routing, replacing the earlier `event_type`-prefix-derivation convention.

actor.description: Who caused the underlying event. Wire shape matches what the bridge stages to `inbox.jsonl`.

id.description: Raw provider-side actor identifier. For kanban: integer user_id as a string. For GitHub: the immutable numeric `sender.id` as a string (not the renameable login). May be null for system-emitted events.

name.description: Registry-resolved friendly name (from the registry built by scanning per-agent YAML `identity` blocks). Null if the actor isn't registered.

is_known_agent.description: True iff the actor matched a registered agent (an agent's `identity` ids in its `<agent>.yml`). Echo suppression already filters out the consumer's own actor; consumers receive this flag for visibility into other registered agents.

summary.description: Human-readable one-line description, ready to render in an inbox surface. Producer's classifier owns the format.

payload.description: Intent-specific structured data. Schema is per-`kind`; consumers should branch on `kind` before accessing keys here. A plain JSON object on the wire (a plain associative array in memory; no freeze/thaw).

ts.description: Unix timestamp (seconds, fractional) stamped when the intent is written to `inbox.jsonl`. `bridge:inbox` uses it for cursor advance. Consumers should use this for ordering; not for wall-clock authority.
